title: GoHighLevel Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**GoHighLevel (HighLevel / LeadConnector)** is an all-in-one CRM, marketing-automation, sales pipeline + multi-channel messaging platform exposing a REST API under `https://services.leadconnectorhq.com`. This connector — `GoHighLevelConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "gohighlevel"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a HighLevel sub-account (location):

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Locations | `/locations` | List + read sub-accounts |
| Contacts | `/contacts` | List, get, create, update, delete |
| Opportunities | `/opportunities` | List, get, create, update CRM opportunities |
| Conversations | `/conversations` | List, get + send messages (SMS/Email/IG/FB) |
| Calendars | `/calendars` | List calendars |
| Pipelines | `/opportunities/pipelines` | List sales pipelines + stages |
| Users | `/users` | List users in a location |
| Campaigns | `/campaigns` | List marketing campaigns |
| Custom Fields | `/locations/{id}/customFields` | List location custom fields |
| Tags | `/locations/{id}/tags` | List location tags |

The connector normalises contacts + opportunities + conversations into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{connector_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::GoHighLevelHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a HighLevel API key

1. Sign in to https://app.gohighlevel.com.
2. For a **location** key: open the location → **Settings → Business Profile → API Key → Generate**.
3. For an **agency** key: **Agency Settings → API Key → Generate**.
4. Copy the API key — HighLevel shows the full token once.
5. (Optional) Copy the **Location ID** from the URL of the location, or from **Settings → Company Info**.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → GoHighLevel**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 4 |
| `location_id` (text, optional) | Default location — methods accept override per call |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://services.leadconnectorhq.com` |
| `api_version` (text, optional) | Defaults to `2021-07-28` (current GA contract) |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is non-empty (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `GoHighLevelHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()`. With `location_id` set, it probes `GET /locations/{id}`; otherwise it lists 1 location. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429/5xx → retried with exponential backoff.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
GoHighLevel REST API uses **server-to-server API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance for this code path, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <api_key>`.
- `Version` is a **mandatory** header on every request (defaults to `2021-07-28`).
- `locationId` is a query/body field on most resource endpoints — sent automatically from the install field when calls omit it.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`. Initialises `GoHighLevelHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any HighLevel endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` wrapping the api_key — no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /locations/{location_id}` if set, else `GET /locations/search?limit=1`. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_key>
Version:        <api_version>      ← defaults to 2021-07-28
Content-Type:   application/json
Accept:         application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | Authorization Bearer token |
| `location_id` | text | no | `self.location_id` | Default location for site-scoped calls |
| `api_version` | text | no | `self.api_version` (default `2021-07-28`) | `Version` header value |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (default `https://services.leadconnectorhq.com`) | Override host for proxies / staging |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_GHL_BASE = "https://services.leadconnectorhq.com"
_DEFAULT_API_VERSION = "2021-07-28"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `GoHighLevelConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::GoHighLevelHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; initialise `GoHighLevelHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a TokenInfo wrapping the api_key.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /locations/{location_id}` if set, otherwise `GET /locations/search?limit=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates HighLevel contacts + opportunities + conversations → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Locations
children.content:
### `async list_locations(*, limit=20, skip=0) -> Dict`
`GET /locations/search?limit={N}&skip={S}`.

### `async get_location(location_id) -> Dict`
`GET /locations/{locationId}`.

children.title: Contacts
children.content:
### `async list_contacts(*, location_id=None, limit=20, page=1, query=None) -> Dict`
`GET /contacts/?locationId=&limit=&page=&query=`.

### `async get_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`GET /contacts/{contactId}`.

### `async create_contact(payload) -> Dict`
`POST /contacts/` — install-field `location_id` injected when payload omits `locationId`.

### `async update_contact(contact_id, payload) -> Dict`
`PUT /contacts/{contactId}`.

### `async delete_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /contacts/{contactId}`.

children.title: Opportunities
children.content:
### `async list_opportunities(*, location_id=None, pipeline_id=None, limit=20, page=1) -> Dict`
`GET /opportunities/search?location_id=&pipeline_id=&limit=&page=`.

### `async get_opportunity(opportunity_id) -> Dict`
`GET /opportunities/{opportunityId}`.

### `async create_opportunity(payload) -> Dict`
`POST /opportunities/` — install-field `location_id` injected when omitted.

### `async update_opportunity(opportunity_id, payload) -> Dict`
`PUT /opportunities/{opportunityId}`.

children.title: Conversations
children.content:
### `async list_conversations(*, location_id=None, contact_id=None, limit=20) -> Dict`
`GET /conversations/search?locationId=&contactId=&limit=`.

### `async get_conversation(conversation_id) -> Dict`
`GET /conversations/{conversationId}`.

### `async send_message(conversation_id, payload) -> Dict`
`POST /conversations/messages` — `conversationId` is injected from the path arg. Payload carries `type` (SMS/Email/FB/IG), `message`, and `contactId`.

children.title: Calendars · Pipelines · Users · Campaigns · Custom Fields · Tags
children.content:
### `async list_calendars(*, location_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /calendars/?locationId=`.

### `async list_pipelines(*, location_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /opportunities/pipelines?locationId=`.

### `async list_users(*, location_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /users/?locationId=`.

### `async list_campaigns(*, location_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /campaigns/?locationId=`.

### `async list_custom_fields(location_id) -> Dict`
`GET /locations/{locationId}/customFields`.

### `async list_tags(location_id) -> Dict`
`GET /locations/{locationId}/tags`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
GoHighLevelError                         # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── GoHighLevelAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── GoHighLevelBadRequestError            # 400 / 422
├── GoHighLevelNotFoundError              # 404
├── GoHighLevelConflictError              # 409
├── GoHighLevelRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
└── GoHighLevelServerError                # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
GoHighLevelNetworkError = GoHighLevelServerError
GoHighLevelNotFound     = GoHighLevelNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `GoHighLevelNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, FAILED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Re-paste the key. HighLevel only shows the full secret once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from HighLevel — key lacks scope or is for the wrong sub-account | Regenerate at the **location** where you need access; or use an agency key if you need cross-location reach. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — API key revoked / wrong header | Verify `Authorization: Bearer ...` is sent (the client does this automatically; check `api_key` install field for stray whitespace). |
| `gohighlevel.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (HighLevel per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Reduce concurrency or lower `rate_limit_per_min`. |
| 404 on a location-scoped call | Wrong `location_id` or contact deleted | Verify the location_id against `list_locations()`; confirm contact exists. |
| `Version header missing` error from HighLevel | `api_version` install field was cleared | Reset to `2021-07-28` — HighLevel rejects requests without a Version header. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'GoHighLevelNetworkError'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `GoHighLevelNetworkError = GoHighLevelServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
